methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Oxford Quantum Circuits is a Series B quantum computing company that has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 11 comparable companies across quantum computing: three public equities (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and eight private firms including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and IonQ.

The Stage-Progression method is the only applicable triangulation approach. Using the $100M total raised as a baseline proxy for current valuation and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, this method yields a point estimate of $190M. A peer-median approach could not be executed due to insufficient narrow comparables; while seven quantum computing peers exist in the dataset, fewer than three match the combination of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier criteria required for statistical reliability.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $162M to $218M (median $190M), derived by stress-testing the stage-progression result ±15%. Key uncertainties include the absence of same-stage and adjacent-stage public or private comparables with disclosed valuations, which limits cross-validation of the multiplier assumption. The $100M raised figure serves as a proxy for current valuation rather than a confirmed pre-money figure, introducing estimation risk. Resolution of these gaps—particularly access to recent Series B+ quantum computing valuations—would strengthen conviction in this range.
